steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the overall purpose of the passage. The passage shows Max has long begged his father to take him to the fortress La Reina, imagines being a hero among friends for crossing its gates, and hopes this summer will finally be the year.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'To explain why Max doesn't want to grow up yet.': The passage never suggests reluctance about growing up — Max's age ("almost twelve") is background detail, not a theme. ✗
  • Choice B — 'To portray how proud Max's father is of Max.': Papá is skeptical ("didn't believe in ghosts") and uninvolved with Max's wish — no pride is shown. ✗
  • Choice C — 'To show how much Max wants to visit La Reina.': Every detail supports this — "for as long as he could remember," "begged," the hero fantasy, "finally." ✓
  • Choice D — 'To criticize Max for disliking summer.': Max is hopeful about summer, not critical of it. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
